results.title: REST list endpoints return non-deterministic results across identical calls â€” even with `orderBy` on a sortable field and a `gte` filter
results.summary: Min Jaccard across 10 pairs = 0.000 (1.0 means identical sets). Identical calls â€” same skip/limit, same orderBy on id (which sorts correctly), same bare-`gte` filter on createdDate (which filters correctly) â€” returned different ID sets.

results.title: MongoDB-style operators (`$gte`) silently ignored; bare operators (`gte`) honored
results.summary: `$gte` is silently dropped (filtered total 5725 = baseline 5725); bare `gte` correctly returns 0 rows for a far-future date. Doc page describes `where` only as type "any" with no examples, so partners can't tell which syntax is right.

results.title: `orderBy` is honored on `id` but not on `createdDate`; `collate=true` does not change behavior
results.summary: orderBy={"id":"asc"} produced 2/49 alphabetic inversions on UUIDs (effectively sorted). Same syntax on createdDate produced â‰Ą22/49 adjacent-pair inversions across all 8 encodings tested. createdDate is not orderable. collate=true: 24 inversions.

results.title: `offset` parameter silently ignored on /customer; `skip` works
results.summary: offset=0 vs offset=50 returned identical IDs (offset dropped). skip=0 vs skip=50 returned disjoint pages (skip works).

results.title: `limit` parameter silently clamped at 100
results.summary: Requested limit=500, received 100 rows (clamped to 100). No error, no warning.

results.title: POST /integration/payment/search â€” `$gte` silently ignored, `gte` honored
results.summary: `$gte` is silently dropped (total 3359 = baseline 3359); bare `gte` correctly returns 0 for far-future and 221 for 2026-04-01T00:00:00Z. Same silent-ignore pattern as test 02.

results.title: Subcontract field renamed: `wholesaleCostCents` no longer present; `costCents` is the actual key
results.summary: Scanned 7 subcontracts across 800 orders. costCents present on 7 (populated on 7). wholesaleCostCents present on 0 (populated on 0). retailCostCents populated on 7.
